title: Facebook's week from hell, Ellen Pao on sexism in Elizabeth Holmes coverage, Newsom's win, frauds & more
description:
Show Notes:

0:00 Besties intro, TPB Symposium recap, rapid generations in tech

7:00 UBI, incentives for success, social safety nets

19:45 Newsom's recall victory in California, where the GOP went wrong

33:59 Facebook's week from hell, Instagram's harmful impact on teen girls

42:42 How to properly regulate social media's impact on certain groups

1:04:46 Pentagon admits to killing 10 Afghan civilians (including seven children) in drone strike

1:07:17 Ellen Pao on sexism around the Elizabeth Holmes trial; Juicero, JUUL, and other male-led failures/frauds

1:25:54 Mailchimp sells for $12B, employees got no equity

1:29:43 AOC at the Met Gala

analysis.summary: The episode covers various topics including Facebook's week from hell, Ellen Pao on sexism in Elizabeth Holmes coverage, Newsom's win, and frauds. The hosts discuss the impact of social media on teen girls and the need for regulation. They also talk about the Pentagon's admission of killing Afghan civilians and the sale of Mailchimp for $12B.

takeaways.text: Facebook knows Instagram is toxic for teen girls
Pentagon admits to killing 10 Afghan civilians
Ellen Pao discusses sexism in Elizabeth Holmes trial
Mailchimp sells for $12B, employees got no equity
Juicero and JUUL are examples of male-led failures

sentiment.notes: The hosts discuss various negative topics, including Facebook's toxic impact on teen girls and the Pentagon's admission of killing Afghan civilians, leading to a cautious sentiment.

notable_moments.description: Ellen Pao discusses sexism in Elizabeth Holmes trial
